title: Marginaria sanctae-rosae
intro: Marginaria sanctae-rosae, also known as the "Rosy Crassulacean", is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family, native to the Cape Provinces of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, anti-inflammatory effects, and antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Marginaria sanctae-rosae include flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Marginaria sanctae-rosae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and treat various skin conditions.
taxonomy: Marginaria sanctae-rosae belongs to the family Crassulaceae, which is a large and diverse group of succulent plants. This species is a type of stonecrop, a common name for plants within the Crassulaceae family. Marginaria sanctae-rosae is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a low-growing, spreading plant with thick, fleshy leaves and produces white or pink flowers in the spring. The classification of Marginaria sanctae-rosae is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Saxifragales.
